_README: IMPORTANT FOR CLAUDE CODE: All terminal nodes (autoAdvance: null, empty options) MUST dismiss on click. Nodes with itemGiven MUST also dismiss on click after the item is added. These are the same patterns that caused freezes in the Lobby.

pf_open.speaker: priya
pf_open.text: You must be the civic tech fellow. Voss mentioned you. I'm Priya Kapoor, UX Research.

pf_casey_intro.speaker: casey
pf_casey_intro.text: Casey Park. Director Voss told me to find you.

pf_priya_everyone.speaker: priya
pf_priya_everyone.text: She tells everyone to find me. I'm starting to think she just doesn't want to deal with the new people herself.

options.text: I saw your whiteboard. You've mapped the entire application process. That's impressive.
I'm a Solutions Engineer. I'm here to build, not just study. What's broken?
The dashboard in the hall says 47-day processing time and a 12,000 application backlog. That's not a backlog, that's a crisis.
The dashboard in the hall says 47-day processing time and a 12,000 application backlog. That's not a backlog, that's a crisis.

pf_whiteboard_path.speaker: priya
pf_whiteboard_path.text: You actually looked at it. Most people walk right past. That board represents six months of user research that nobody asked for and nobody funded.

pf_casey_47_steps.speaker: casey
pf_casey_47_steps.text: 47 steps to process one application. Who designed this?

pf_coral_reef.speaker: priya
pf_coral_reef.text: Nobody 'designed' it. It evolved. Like a coral reef, except ugly and it hurts people.

pf_direct_path.speaker: priya
pf_direct_path.text: 'Here to build.' I've heard that before. The last engineer spent three months writing a requirements document and then their fellowship ended.

pf_casey_code.speaker: casey
pf_casey_code.text: I don't write requirements documents. I write code.

pf_dashboard_path_mrs_g.speaker: priya
pf_dashboard_path_mrs_g.text: You said crisis. Not 'opportunity.' Not 'challenge.' Crisis. That's the right word.

pf_casey_12000_mrs_g.speaker: casey
pf_casey_12000_mrs_g.text: 12,000 people waiting. That's not abstract. Those are real people like the woman I met in the lobby.

pf_priya_lobby.speaker: priya
pf_priya_lobby.text: You met someone in the lobby?

pf_casey_mrs_g.speaker: casey
pf_casey_mrs_g.text: Mrs. Gutierrez. She's submitted the same documents every month for her benefits review. The system keeps losing them.

pf_priya_dolores.speaker: priya
pf_priya_dolores.text: I know Dolores. She's one of my research participants. She showed you her folder, didn't she?

pf_dashboard_path_no_mrs_g.speaker: priya
pf_dashboard_path_no_mrs_g.text: You said crisis. Not 'opportunity.' Not 'challenge.' Crisis. That's the right word.

pf_casey_12000_no_mrs_g.speaker: casey
pf_casey_12000_no_mrs_g.text: 12,000 people waiting for help they were promised. That's not a number. That's a crisis.

pf_converge_level.speaker: priya
pf_converge_level.text: Look, I'll level with you. I've been here two years. I know what's wrong. I've documented it. I've presented it. What I haven't had is an engineer who stays long enough to actually fix it.

pf_casey_computer.speaker: casey
pf_casey_computer.text: I'm not going anywhere. But first things first — I need a computer. My desk is completely empty.

pf_priya_supply.speaker: priya
pf_priya_supply.text: Of course it is. Welcome to D.A.S.H. There are laptops in the supply closet, but it's keypad locked and the only person who knew the code retired last year.

options.text: Can we just break into it?

pf_break_in.speaker: priya
pf_break_in.text: A federal employee breaking into government property on day one. That's a bold strategy. I respect it, but no.

pf_code_path.speaker: priya
pf_code_path.text: I figured it out about a year ago. Tried every four-digit combination that a GS-12 IT specialist would use as a password. Got it on the third try.

pf_priya_1997.speaker: priya
pf_priya_1997.text: Close. 1997. The year the system was deployed. But I'm not just giving it to you.

pf_priya_prove.speaker: priya
pf_priya_prove.text: Because the last person I helped got a laptop, spent a week on it, and then used it exclusively for writing status update emails. Show me you're going to actually do something and the code is yours.

paw_open.speaker: casey
paw_open.text: Steps 12 through 17 are redundant. The system asks for documents it already has because the stages don't share a database.

paw_priya_react.speaker: priya
paw_priya_react.text: ...you saw that in five minutes? I spent three weeks getting leadership to understand that.

pap_open.speaker: priya
pap_open.text: You fixed the printer. That thing has been jammed for two weeks and nobody bothered. You've been here an hour and you already solved a problem nobody else would touch.

pap_priya_rare.speaker: priya
pap_priya_rare.text: No. But it showed you're the kind of person who sees a broken thing and fixes it instead of filing a ticket. That's rare here.

pgc_open.speaker: priya
pgc_open.text: Okay. You actually did the thing. That's more than anyone else has managed on day one.

pgc_priya_code.speaker: priya
pgc_priya_code.text: 1-9-9-7. Go get yourself a laptop. And when you're set up, come find me. We've got 89 days left and a lot of work to do.

pd_open.speaker: priya
pd_open.text: Go set up your laptop. We've got work to do.
